q:
You are driving your car in a bicycle zone.
Are you allowed to overtake a step?
e:
LESSON 3 - 'Cyclists'
In a bicycle zone, motor vehicles are not allowed to overtake cyclists or users of mobility devices (such as electric steps), even if there is enough space.
The maximum speed is limited to 30 km/h.

You are driving your car in a bicycle zone.

clean_e:
LESSON 3 - 'Cyclists'
In a bicycle zone, motor vehicles are not allowed to overtake cyclists or users of mobility devices (such as electric steps), even if there is enough space.
The maximum speed is limited to 30 km/h.

q:
Which drivers are not allowed to drive on the cycle lane past this sign?

A. Only mopeds class B.
B. Mopeds class A and B.
e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
If the pedestrians and bicycles are not next to each other, but among each other, then only cyclists and pedestrians use this part of the public road.
clean_q:
Which drivers are not allowed to drive on the cycle lane past this sign? 
A. Only mopeds class B.
B. Mopeds class A and B.
clean_e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
If the pedestrians and bicycles are not next to each other, but among each other, then only cyclists and pedestrians use this part of the public road.

q:
This girl is 8 years old. She is cycling on the roadway within a built-up area.
May she also ride on the raised pavement?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 3
If there is a cycle path, a cyclist must use it (unless it is not rideable).
It is forbidden to cycle on the pavement within a built-up area, except for children under 10, who may always ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.
Outside built-up areas, cyclists may, if there is no usable cycle path and provided they ride on the right-hand side of the road, ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.

This girl is 8 years old. She is cycling on the roadway within a built-up area.

A. Yes. 
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 3
If there is a cycle path, a cyclist must use it (unless it is not rideable).
It is forbidden to cycle on the pavement within a built-up area, except for children under 10, who may always ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.
Outside built-up areas, cyclists may, if there is no usable cycle path and provided they ride on the right-hand side of the road, ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.

q: Can you overtake a speed pedelec with your car in a bike zone?
e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
Drivers of motor vehicles (eg cars, vans, trucks, mopeds class A and B) are NOT allowed to overtake cyclists and speed pedelecs in a bike zone.
clean_q: Can you overtake a speed pedelec with your car in a bike zone?
clean_e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
Drivers of motor vehicles (eg cars, vans, trucks, mopeds class A and B) are NOT allowed to overtake cyclists and speed pedelecs in a bike zone.

q:
Is the blue car allowed to use the cycle lane a bit in order to overtake on the right, the car that wants to turn left?

A. No.
B. If he doesn’t hinder cyclists.
C. Yes, but not more than 50% of the cycle lane.
e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
A cycle lane is a part of the public road, reserved for the use of bicycles and 2-wheeled class A mopeds (max 25 km/hour).
You are not allowed to drive your car on a cycle lane and you are not allowed to park or wait to let a passenger in or out of your car.

LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.

The maximum speed limit = 30 km/hour.
In a bike zone cars are not allowed to overtake the cyclist.

q: Are you allowed to overtake a moped on the left with a car in a bicycle zone?
e:
In a bicycle zone, motor vehicles are not allowed to overtake a cyclist, scooter, or any other means of transport considered equivalent to a bicycle.
Other vehicles (for example, a moped) may be overtaken on the left in a bicycle zone, provided that the maximum permitted speed is not exceeded.

Other vehicles (for example, a moped) may be overtaken on the left in a bicycle zone, provided that the maximum permitted speed is not exceeded.

q:
The cycle lane ends and the moped driver must drive onto the road.

A. The moped driver has to give way to the drivers on the road.
B. The drivers on the road must give way to the driver of the moped.
e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
If a warning/danger sign says that the bicycle lane ends, they have right of way and therefore the drivers of cars and motor vehicles on the road have to give priority.

q:
Which vehicles are you not allowed to overtake with your car after this sign?

A. Only a cyclist.
B. Only a cyclist and a class A moped.
C. A cyclist, a class A moped and a motorcycle.
e:
LESSON 3
This sign marks the beginning of a bicycle zone.
In a bicycle zone, motor vehicles (including mopeds) are not allowed to overtake cyclists, speed pedelecs and bicycles up to 1 m wide.
Other vehicles may be overtaken if they are driving too slowly.
The maximum permitted speed in a bicycle zone is 30 km/h.
clean_q:
Which vehicles are you not allowed to overtake with your car after this sign?
A. Only a cyclist.
B. Only a cyclist and a class A moped.
C. A cyclist, a class A moped and a motorcycle.
clean_e:
LESSON 3
This sign marks the beginning of a bicycle zone.
In a bicycle zone, motor vehicles (including mopeds) are not allowed to overtake cyclists, speed pedelecs and bicycles up to 1 m wide.
Other vehicles may be overtaken if they are driving too slowly.
The maximum permitted speed in a bicycle zone is 30 km/h.

q: May a user of a motorized personal mobility device be overtaken by motor vehicles in a bicycle zone?
e:
LESSON 3
Users of motorized personal mobility devices are treated the same as cyclists.
In a bicycle zone, cyclists may not be overtaken by motor vehicles. This overtaking ban also applies to motorcycles and mopeds of class A and B. The speed pedelec, however, is considered a bicycle and may overtake other cyclists.
On the other hand, cyclists may overtake each other and other road users. Overtaking must always be done on the left. A cyclist may also pass stationary vehicles on the right.
Priority vehicles (police, fire brigade, ambulance with siren and flashing light) are still allowed to overtake cyclists. If a priority vehicle is approaching, clear the roadway.
clean_q: May a user of a motorized personal mobility device be overtaken by motor vehicles in a bicycle zone?
clean_e:
LESSON 3
Users of motorized personal mobility devices are treated the same as cyclists.
In a bicycle zone, cyclists may not be overtaken by motor vehicles. This overtaking ban also applies to motorcycles and mopeds of class A and B. The speed pedelec, however, is considered a bicycle and may overtake other cyclists.
On the other hand, cyclists may overtake each other and other road users. Overtaking must always be done on the left. A cyclist may also pass stationary vehicles on the right.
Priority vehicles (police, fire brigade, ambulance with siren and flashing light) are still allowed to overtake cyclists. If a priority vehicle is approaching, clear the roadway.

q:
In this bicycle zone, the boy is riding an e-scooter at 20 km/h.
Is the driver of the car allowed to overtake the e-scooter on the left?

A. Yes, because it is not a cyclist.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 3
A bicycle zone starts at the “begin bicycle zone” traffic sign and ends at the “end bicycle zone” traffic sign.
In a bicycle zone:
• cyclists, speed pedelecs and personal mobility devices (such as an e-scooter) may use the roadway;
• motor vehicles are allowed to drive there, but they are not allowed to overtake cyclists, e-scooters or similar vehicles;
• the maximum speed is 30 km/h.
➡️ Even if the e-scooter is riding at 20 km/h, overtaking is not allowed.
clean_q:
In this bicycle zone, the boy is riding an e-scooter at 20 km/h.
Is the driver of the car allowed to overtake the e-scooter on the left?
A. Yes, because it is not a cyclist.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 3
A bicycle zone starts at the “begin bicycle zone” traffic sign and ends at the “end bicycle zone” traffic sign.
In a bicycle zone:
• cyclists, speed pedelecs and personal mobility devices (such as an e-scooter) may use the roadway;
• motor vehicles are allowed to drive there, but they are not allowed to overtake cyclists, e-scooters or similar vehicles;
• the maximum speed is 30 km/h.
➡️ Even if the e-scooter is riding at 20 km/h, overtaking is not allowed.

q:
From how many cyclists in a group, are these cyclists not obliged to ride on the bicycle lane?

A. From 10.
B. From 15.
C. From 20.
e:
LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
Groups of 15 to 50 cyclists are not obliged to follow the cycle paths. They are allowed to drive with two next to each other on the roadway. Provided they stay together (grouped). Groups of less than 15 cyclists must follow the same rules as individual cyclists.

q:
This girl is 14 years old. There is no cycle path.
May she ride on the pavement?

A. Yes, only within a built-up area.
B. Yes, only outside a built-up area.
C. Never.
e:
LESSON 3
If there is a cycle path, a cyclist must use it (unless it is not rideable).
It is forbidden to cycle on the pavement within a built-up area, except for children under 10, who may always ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.
Outside built-up areas, cyclists may, if there is no usable cycle path and provided they ride on the right-hand side of the road, ride on the pavement or on the raised verge.

LESSON 3 – ‘The bicycle lane’.
A bicycle reserved/suggested lane is not a bicycle lane at all.
It is nothing else but a painted part of the road, where you can drive, park or wait.
